manager_bullets: Next steps at end of calls are consistently missing. Conversations ending without commitments means deals risk going dark.
Strongest area: sharing specific examples of past work. This is landing well and building credibility.
Problem surfacing needs attention. Coming into conversations with vague asks rather than sharp, specific questions.

listening_ratio.quote: I've sort of built a system to help sales reps write super personalized emails at scale... I'm at the position where I'm reaching out to my network...
listening_ratio.feedback: Good listening once Nathan started talking, but opened with five sentences of context before asking the first question. Lead with a question next time to get into listening mode immediately.

problem_surfacing.quote: I just wanted to see any advice, tips, things like that.
problem_surfacing.feedback: Came in with a vague ask. The best insight of the call came from Nathan, not from a sharp question. Come in with one specific decision to pressure-test next time.

credibility_moments.quote: I built a dashboard in 10 minutes with live artifacts. Just like, pulling in call data, pipeline, how each rep is doing.
credibility_moments.feedback: Strong. Concrete examples landed well and shifted the conversation to peer level. Do this earlier and it will set a better tone for the whole call.

next_step_clarity.quote: All right, thanks Nathan. I appreciate the time. This has been fantastic.
next_step_clarity.feedback: Call ended with nothing concrete. Always close with a specific action, even a small one. It keeps the relationship open and shows you took the call seriously.

curiosity_unlocked.quote: How was that journey for you in terms of learning all that stuff? Were you technical before?
curiosity_unlocked.feedback: Nathan leaned in naturally and asked questions back, but this happened on its own rather than being engineered. Use specific examples as invitations to pull the other person in.
